roadmapSummary: Implement deterministic context-pack generation, task-specific context selection, context budget regression fixtures, and provider-neutral budget/model routing for Kyro.

sizingDecision.rationale: The remaining cost work adds a new public CLI surface, task-specific context selection, regression fixtures, and a separate model/budget routing contract. Splitting keeps the public command foundation, task-pack behavior, and budget-routing policy independently reviewable.
splitTriggers: New public CLI command and options need isolated integration tests.
Task-specific context packs depend on the base context-pack contract and need separate fixtures.
Model/budget routing affects product policy and documentation independently from context-pack mechanics.
sizingDecision.whyNotFewer: Two sprints would mix CLI command plumbing, task-specific extraction, fixture budgets, and model routing policy into large review units with unclear rollback boundaries.
sizingDecision.whyNotMore: Four or more sprints would add orchestration overhead without a new dependency boundary; docs and model routing can be completed together after context-pack fixtures exist.
sprintProofs: Sprint 1 is independently provable when `kyro context-pack --kyro-scope <scope>` emits a bounded summary-first package for existing scopes with tests.
Sprint 2 is independently provable when `--task <id>` context packs include only task-relevant details, rules, and validation criteria with budget regression fixtures.
Sprint 3 is independently provable when budget/model routing is encoded in config/types/docs and validated without provider-specific model names.
